# ADR-260003: Public-site information architecture and content layout

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-21
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

The project has confirmed a static public site at the root domain and a separate SaaS app at
`app.crystord.com`. The content-curation decisions are already recorded, but the site-level content
structure (home sections, standalone pages, footer, and sign-in language) must be locked to avoid
drift during implementation.

## Decision

- Use a multi-page public-site model with a focused landing page and dedicated detail pages.
- Landing-page section order:
  1. Hero (value proposition)
  2. Primary actions (Sign in to Crystord App, Explore Google Add-on)
  3. Product overview (3-5 key capabilities)
  4. Trust/compliance strip (links to Privacy, Terms, Support)
  5. Connect block (Contact, YouTube)
  6. Final call-to-action
- Keep standalone pages for Google Add-on, Add-on Privacy, Add-on Terms, Add-on Support, and Contact.
- Footer navigation includes Google Add-on, Support, Privacy, Terms, Contact, and YouTube.
- Primary sign-in wording is `Sign in to Crystord App`, targeting the app domain.

## Alternatives Considered

- Single-page site only: Rejected because legal/support/add-on content becomes harder to maintain
  and less discoverable.
- Separate sign-in page as the main entry point: Rejected because sign-in is intentionally embedded
  in the landing experience while still redirecting to the app domain.
- Minimal footer with only legal links: Rejected because support/contact discoverability is a core
  objective for the public site.

## Consequences

- Positive: Clear navigation and separation between marketing information and reference/legal pages.
- Positive: Better discoverability for support and compliance content.
- Negative: More explicit page ownership and content maintenance overhead.
- Risk: If navigation labels diverge from the accepted wording, user clarity can regress.

## Verification

- Home page contains all agreed sections in the defined order.
- Top-level navigation and footer include all required links.
- Standalone pages exist for Add-on, Privacy, Terms, Support, and Contact.
- Sign-in control text uses the accepted wording and routes to the app domain.

## Rollback

If the structure underperforms, revise via a follow-up ADR and update associated requirement and
backlog records before implementation changes.
